text.title: Freelance Marketplace Platform Tradeoffs Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of Freelance Marketplace Platform Tradeoffs MUST enforce. Rationale + source per rule. Rules referenced by id from `content/06-decision-tree.xml`.

rule.statement: Each platform MUST be scored on fee / lead-quality / exclusivity / ranking / density. Skipping any axis invalidates the decision.
rule.rationale: Single-axis evaluation (fee-only) is the #1 reason freelancers pick wrong platform; five-axis catches lock-in and churn risks.

rule.statement: Selected platform MUST match the freelancer's rate band per published platform medians. Mismatch >= 30% requires switch.
rule.rationale: Platforms strongly cluster around rate-band medians; mismatched freelancers either price-floor or starve for leads.

rule.statement: Exclusivity clauses MUST be flagged in the decision record. Hidden exclusivity terminates the methodology output as invalid.
rule.rationale: Toptal / Braintrust exclusivity restrict outside work; freelancers regularly sign without reading.

rule.statement: Decision MUST include a primary AND a secondary platform (or explicit reason for primary-only). Platform monoculture is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Single-platform freelancers are one ban or algorithm change from zero income; secondary platform is risk hedge.

rule.statement: Decision record MUST list >=2 churn triggers (when to leave the platform). Indefinite stays without exit criteria are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Freelancers overstay platforms; defined exits force re-evaluation at known thresholds.

rule.statement: If applicability preconditions are unmet (see AGENTS.md Skip If), the methodology MUST be skipped and a one-line skip-reason logged.
rule.rationale: A named skip terminal keeps decision-tree leaves resolvable and prevents silent misuse on misfit inputs.
